page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the ARM64-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with one thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs of dedicated compute capacity. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The CPU cache architecture includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several key metrics. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1519.79 and a multi-core score of 12130.94. Memory bandwidth is measured at 472.59 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 119.78 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression fall into the weaker performance tier. Cryptographic capabilities are supported via hardware AES, though the instance lacks AVX, AVX512, and AMX instruction sets.

The c6gn.2xlarge is network and EBS-optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This high network throughput relative to its compute profile makes it highly cost-efficient for network-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Based on benchmark results, the instance is well-suited for static web serving, Redis database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for large language model inference or heavy text processing tasks due to weaker performance in those areas.
description: The Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies entirely on EBS for storage. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and static web serving capabilities in the average tier, while single-threaded compression and LLM inference speeds are relatively weak. With its dedicated cores and high network-to-compute ratio, the c6gn.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for network-intensive, compute-aligned workloads. It is best suited for applications such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and distributed build systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB of RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance with dedicated cores and high network bandwidth for efficient web serving and database operations.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores running at 2.5 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB L3 cache
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Average performance in static web serving and Redis operations
Weaker performance in single-threaded compression and LLM inference
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database workloads
